methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Eon Systems is a Seed-stage biotech company that has raised $3.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 companies across Biotech & Health (3 public, 8 private), including comparable-stage entrants like Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics.

Given limited narrow-peer data at identical stage and valuation tier, only the Stage-Progression method yielded a reliable signal. Using total capital raised ($3.5M, rounded to $4M as a proxy for post-money value) as the base and applying a standard 2.5x multiplier for progression from Seed to the next financing round, this approach implies a valuation of $9M. The peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables in the Biotech & Health sector (fewer than three datapoints met filter criteria). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples was not feasible without revenue disclosure from the target.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $7M to $10M, centered on $9M. The median reflects the single applicable method. Key uncertainties include the absence of disclosed revenue (precluding public multiples calibration), limited same-stage private peer granularity, and the appropriateness of the 2.5x step-up multiplier to Eon's specific market conditions and capital efficiency. Further diligence on comparable financings and unit economics would strengthen conviction in this range.
